level_signals: Partners with senior GTM leaders at enterprise accounts
Expected to build programs from scratch (0 → 1)
Described as 'strategic business partner, trusted advisor'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strategic, product-minded thinking: You don't just solve today's problem: you see around corners, help customers envision what's next, and connect their roadmap to ours.
Deep GTM fluency: You've lived in B2B SaaS and can speak the language of revops, sales, and marketing leaders.
Customer obsession with business savvy: You've worked directly with customers (not just managed accounts) and you know how to build trust, challenge thinking, and deliver impact.
Builder DNA: You thrive in ambiguity, love creating structure where none exists, and get energy from designing processes and teams from 0 → 1.
Passion for Clay: You believe in our mission and are excited to evangelize Clay as if it were your own product.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bonus if you've held an in-house GTM operator role.

scope.surface: Enterprise customer success and growth strategy for Clay's largest SaaS accounts
core_responsibilities: Guide customers to first 'aha' moment in Clay, ensuring fast time-to-value
Act as an extension of customers' GTM teams by co-creating new plays and uncovering fresh use cases
Continuously identify opportunities for Clay across sales, revops, marketing, and CX
Drive strong renewals and multi-team expansions by delivering outsized value
Synthesize patterns from enterprise accounts and collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Design
Design scalable frameworks, playbooks, and initiatives for future Enterprise customers

compensation.equity_form: Community equity round mentioned; employee tender offer referenced

green_flags: Company has crossed $100M ARR and raised $100M Series C — strong financial signals
$5B valuation with recent employee tender offer suggests liquidity path
Named enterprise customers are top-tier (Anthropic, Notion, Google, Ramp, Intercom, Verkada)
Role has genuine strategic scope — roadmap influence, building from scratch
Community and culture appear differentiated (coaches, operating principles, diverse backgrounds)
red_flags: Location and remote/hybrid policy not stated anywhere in the JD
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
No years-of-experience requirements given — seniority bar is unclear
Role title includes 'Customer Success' but responsibilities read more like enterprise sales/strategy — potential scope ambiguity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope and partner seniority, not stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred from company-level equity round mention, not role-specific)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no location information in JD
ambiguities: Role title says 'Customer Success' but the description positions it as a strategic GTM advisory/growth role, not a traditional CSM — archetype is ambiguous between BizOps/Strategy and Growth (non-PM)
